content: What do you think about using this contact e-mail list here to offer to contribute to the inventory of existing training documents ? or would you prefer we post a message on the google group or zulip? (I'm just afraid that not all installations follow these channels).
I don't mind if you reach out to people directly.

@Sonia Barbosa and others who have given training webinars recently (@Katie Mika @Julian Gautier @Ceilyn Boyd @Gustavo Durand and @Leo Andreev from a quick look at DataverseTV :tv:), this new "training resources" topic came out of today's Documentation working group meeting.

I volunteered to let you know that the meeting was recorded and training resources was the first topic. If you have the time, please check it out! There are notes as well.

In short, there's a lot of interest in inventorying existing resources (doc) and generally collaborating on training material in the future.

Thanks for the ping! I checked out the video and the notes doc. The folks at UNC have made videos about using Dataverse, too. I think they have a YouTube channel and I imagine they'll be pretty responsive if they're contacted.
@Ines Schmahl @Amélie Fiocca and everyone, I just added over 30 links to tutorial from DataverseTV to the doc. Enjoy! :tv: :popcorn:
This is the script I used: training.py

@Amélie Fiocca, on that "Crowdsourced information about Dataverse installations" spreadsheet I added a note on the column header for the "check date" column that you added, so I and maybe others know what it's for. Phil guessed it's related to when you were looking for tutorials from each installation. Feel free to edit the note if I missed or misunderstood anything about how the column's being used
Hi @Julian Gautier , thank you for the note. You're right, it's definitely related to the date of my research for tutorials :)

Hi @Philip, I had a comment her : https://docs.google.com/document/d/1SG2mgq-6lzAw_r0eXRZB6L-_ZcKNfi_1sLPHtcCuKI8/edit?tab=t.0
I could have created a spreadsheet, but I don't think I have the rights to this folder

Hmm, you're a contributor to the parent folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1iLUtHsRw4NYUQoKenpGF6oYb-s23plL6?usp=drive_link

You can't create any docs or spreadsheets in that folder? :thinking:
Ah, that's strange, I'll try again later. I will also suggest a email template to send for the contribution of installations that the group can review.
Sounds perfect! Thanks!

Here is the spreadsheet -> https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1SR6A68dXv4dVpCImR7Gke-ps25xvOsw1_pXwjDxGMXU/edit?gid=0#gid=0
And the email template to review : https://docs.google.com/document/d/1givY39bRVhrcAS6FTJ1PZ5fKzS31keA7TXy4CWo_VEM/edit?tab=t.0
What do you think about it ?
@Amélie Fiocca the spreadsheet looks good, thanks, but the links don't work
I fixed it :)
Looks great! Thanks! What should the permissions on the doc be? Currently it's restricted.

Good question... I suppose we could open it (just this document) or ask to installation write us on this channel with the answer for each field but it will require more work to compile them. Do you agree with the proposed template for the email?
Can you please open it (the spreadsheet), for comment?
I commented on the email template with some thoughts.
We should probably decide how we want people to give us their links to training materials before we send it out.
For instance, we could make the spreadsheet publicly editable but that might be a bit chaotic. :sweat_smile:
I think it's okay, I've set the permissions correctly to open comments publicly.
Great. That's what I do with the community news as I work on it. I leave it open for comments.

Speaking of the community news, I just sent out the July edition and mentioned we'd like help compiling training resources.

We should still email the Google Group, of course. :smile:

Hmm, I just found this: https://docs.scholarsportal.info/view/Main/SP/Borealis/Borealis_Admin/Promotional_and_Training_Materials/

I assume @Amber Leahey @Bikram and others at Borealis know about it. :smile:
New videos from @Courtney Mumma! See #community > more new videos from TDL
A colleague of @Philipp Conzett is asking about datasets for teaching and learning: https://groups.google.com/g/dataverse-community/c/-pEkvJMsoog/m/2IEXnWq9BAAJ
seems related
I just replied: https://groups.google.com/g/dataverse-community/c/-pEkvJMsoog/m/YOiktWmUAQAJ
A message was moved here from #docs > doc group meeting by Philip Durbin 🚀.
@Amélie Fiocca I moved your message to this topic. Sure! Sounds good!

Hi everyone,
To move forward with the topic of training resources, I propose creating a form for installations using the Grist tool.
This will allow me to easily create lists linked to repositories and facilitate the formatting of results.
We could discuss the repositories:

  • type of training,
  • target audience,
  • level?, etc.

An interesting objective would be to have a web page that lists training courses with the ability to search by type, target audience, level, and also by language!
Here are some screenshots of my tests with the Grist tool.
Of course, I can share them publicly in read-only or edit mode. We can also migrate the results.
What do you think?
We can discuss it next time :)
I've never heard of Grist, but I like their tagline "Spreadsheet Software to End Data Chaos" -- https://www.getgrist.com :smile:
It's even open source, apparently: https://github.com/gristlabs/grist-core :eyes:

It's a really great tool.
It's part of a digital suite available to government employees in France. I've started using it to manage quite a few things, and it's really very practical. You can do very simple things quickly, but it's also possible to code formulas in Python to do very complex tasks.
It reminds me a bit of Airtable.
a link to test : https://grist.numerique.gouv.fr/o/docs/wQqzFFsD11Zu/DATAVERSE-DOC?utm_id=share-doc

The idea would be to offer the survey or one option to send a table with the same format for easy integration (in case of multiple resources). I haven't tried it yet, but maybe it's possible to allow users to fill in several resource fields within the same form.
A message was moved here from #docs > docs wg at DCM 2026 in Barcelona by Philip Durbin 🚀.
@Amélie Fiocca I hope you don't mind that I moved your message here.
Overall, the form looks great! Some notes:
typo: ressources should be resources
I see mm/dd/yy and as and American I'm cool with this but I hope others see whatever is most comfortable for them!
"Dataverse installation" is required but what if theirs isn't on the list? :thinking:
Maybe "Contact email" instead of just "Contact"? :thinking:
Yes, that's a lot of languages. :smile: I guess it's fine
I like the link at the bottom to see the full list of training resources.
Good job!

Thanks , I made the corrections :)
For date, I guess that will be okay because the choice is made with the calendar ?
sure, sounds fine :smile:

Amélie Fiocca said:

Here's the form, what do you think?
It includes all the languages ​​of the ISO standard to be as inclusive as possible! :sweat_smile: but maybe it's too much...
Gristform

Then I could generate a QR code like this, for example?

I can just add the qr code in the design right now because you gave the link already, thanks! I'll see how can incorporate it.
The form looks really good to me (not too long ;) ). And I think the date format might be browser decided, because I'm getting dd/mm/yyyy
Oh, good. I was wondering if dd/mm vs mm/dd was decided by the browser. :smile:
Thanks, Dieuwertje :) Can you generate a new QR code to integrate it? cause I don't know if this one will work . It was just an example and I didn't save it.
Or else, we can try to create another one using this tool: https://www.qrcode-monkey.com/fr/
yeah, I can generate a QR code with some color options. I'll also add it as a shorturl at the bottom to ensure everyone can access it if the QR isn't working for some.
